Prompt
U
Oracle Events
- Search Linear for all ExpenseBot project tickets (proj_expense_autocategorizer) to find every open engineering ticket and its current state.
- Read comments on key Linear tickets (issue_stipend_duplicate_detection, issue_pipeline_hardening, issue_edge_cases_backlog, issue_p1_vectral_config, issue_p1_mosaic_malformed, etc.) to determine what work actually shipped vs. what's still stuck.
- Search Slack for ExpenseBot discussions, audit findings, and relaunch conversations to understand the history of concerns raised and what follow-ups occurred.
- Search emails for audit-related threads, Dmitri's fraud findings shared with Marcus, and AM verification emails sent to client HR reps (Mina -> Rachel Whitfield, Emeka -> Tamara Byrd, Emeka -> Tessa Moreno) to discover whether fraud follow-ups received responses.
- Search Airtable for stipend records (via airtable_list_records or airtable_search_records) to check which pilot expense decisions were corrected vs. still wrong discovering that Maria Santos ($550), James Okafor ($82), and Lisa Park ($130) remain rejected.
- Look up Samira Tariq's email address using contacts search.
- Update all ExpenseBot Linear tickets (via linear_update_issue) with real engineering status - mark completed tickets as Done, add status comments to stalled/overdue tickets.
- Add a comment (via linear_create_comment) to the duplicate receipt detection ticket (issue_stipend_duplicate_detection / ENG-220), noting it is still in "To Do" and has not been started.
- Send an email (via send_email) from dmitri.volkov@moveops.com to samira.tariq@moveops.com with the full engineering readiness assessment - what's done, what's still broken, what's blocking relaunch.
- Post a summary (via conversations_add_message) in the #engineering Slack channel with the engineering status for team visibility.

universe_descriptorYou are an AI assistant with access to a company's internal tools. Use the available tools to complete the entire task autonomously. No further user input will be given. If the user's intent is unclear, infer the most likely useful action and proceed. Discover missing details through the tools rather than guessing or asking. The current date is April 26, 2026. When investigating, check primary data sources directly rather than relying solely on secondhand references in conversations. Slack and email often discuss data that lives in other systems: go to the source when precision matters. About MoveOps Inc.: MoveOps is a corporate relocation management company. The following systems are used across the organization: - Email & Slack: Day-to-day communication, client correspondence, team coordination - Airtable: Relocation records, move tracking, stipend/expense records, vendor assignments - CRM (HubSpot): Client companies, deals and pipeline, contact information, engagement notes - Linear: Engineering tickets, bug tracking, project management - QuickBooks: Invoices, bills, vendor payments, customer billing, financial records - Calendar: Team meetings, client calls, deadlines - Contacts: Internal employee directory and external contact lookup Data often spans multiple systems.

12 The final response or email to [samira.tariq@moveops.com](mailto:samira.tariq@moveops.com) (visible in send_email parameters) identifies that the Vectral config fix (ENG-214) and Mosaic config fix (ENG-215) have had their corrections deployed.
outcome
 ✓✓✓✓✓✓ 100%
✓Run #1Both the final response and the email to samira.tariq@moveops.com explicitly identify ENG-214 (Vectral config fix) and ENG-215 (Mosaic config fix) as deployed/Done, confirmed working in production.
✓Run #2The email body explicitly states 'ENG-214 — Vectral config fix ✅ Done — Lena deployed corrected limits on 3/18' and 'ENG-215 — Mosaic config fix ✅ Done — Lena corrected the malformed JSON types... Deployed and verified 3/18.' Both config fixes are identified as deployed/shipped.
✓Run #3Both the final response and the email explicitly state ENG-214 (Vectral config fix) and ENG-215 (Mosaic config fix) were deployed as hotfixes mid-March. The email labels both as 'Done' with notes that corrections were deployed.
✓Run #4The final response states 'ENG-214/215: Vectral and Mosaic config fixes — deployed and verified by Lena on 3/18.' The email also explicitly covers ENG-214 (Vectral config fix, deployed 3/18) and ENG-215 (Mosaic config fix, deployed 3/18) as completed.
